subject: Agricultural Science
coverageNote: Form directories exist only where teaching-module source PDFs were ingested. Missing forms must resolve as known-gap, not empty objects.

lesson.title: 4.0 FARM STRUCTURES AND MACHINERY
activities: Farm Machinery - Tools and machines to carryout farm work.
Simple farm tools operated by hand.
Equipment used with animals or machines to perform farm tasks.
Care and repair of farm tools and machinery.
resources: AGRICULTURAL SCIENCE TEACHING MODULE FORM 2 TERM 1 &
In agriculture, implements are tools or equipment used to carryout farm tasks more efficiently. In this sub-topic, learners will study
animal and tractor drawn implements which are essential for land preparation, and other farm operations. Animal drawn implements
are attached to drought animals like oxen and are used for tasks such as ploughing, harrowing and cultivating small farms. Tractor
assessment: List four environmental factors to consider when choosing a site for farm structures.
AGRICULTURAL SCIENCE TEACHING MODULE FORM 2 TERM 1 &
Sketch a simple farm layout and indicate the most suitable site for livestock shelter.
Explain why site selection is important before constructing farm buildings.
A proposed site is near a low-lying area prone to flooding. Suggest an alternative location to explain your reasoning.
Explain how proper site selection contributes to durability, cost savings and efficient farm management.
